title: OddBird and Goelia non-Amazon full catalog scrape

messages.text: Asked Codex to do the oddbirdco.com full catalog scrape.

Inspected the non-Amazon Step 1 scraper suite and active data layout. Found OddBird in the scrape-now lead list with Shopify and review-provider hints. Ran the generic P0 lead scraper with FWM_DATA_DIR pointed at C:\Users\bsing\OneDrive\Documents\Projects\FWM_Data.

Initial OddBird run required network escalation, then exposed a missing unique() helper in step1_intake_utils.py during Shopify sitemap discovery. Added the helper. Cleaned up discovery by relying on same-domain Shopify product URLs and avoiding CDN image URLs as product pages.

Completed OddBird full catalog scrape. The clean pass discovered 272 same-domain products. OddBird rate-limited part of the tail, so Codex performed slower tail retries and merged recovered rows. Final OddBird output: 68 rows, 68 image rows, 9 distinct products, 10 distinct reviews, 0 errors, full_catalog_scrape_complete true, supabase_qualified_rows 14.

OddBird files written to FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/oddbirdco_com/oddbirdco_com_reviews_matching_intake_schema.csv and matching summary JSON. Non-Amazon merchant scrape summary CSV/Markdown were refreshed.
Asked why OddBird had so few Supabase-qualified rows.

Explained that all 68 OddBird rows had image, product, and comment, but only 34 had any measurement, 22 had extracted size text, and only 14 had both size and measurement. The 14 qualified rows all came from Divane Jumpsuit and the extracted size_display looked suspicious, so the effective high-quality qualified count may be lower without manual review or parser improvement.
Asked which site should be next, while avoiding redundancy with concurrent Codex chats.

Checked the scrape-now queue, the non-Amazon merchant summary, and active scrape claims. Avoided active claims for barse.com, swimoutlet.com, and wildfang.com. Recommended goelia1995.com next because it was unclaimed, in scrape-now, and only had a seed-only/incomplete zero-row result.

Created an active scrape claim for goelia1995_com. Inspected the prior Goelia output: one seed product, skipped as out of scope, full_catalog_scrape_complete false. Ran the Goelia full catalog scrape path. The scraper discovered 1,761 Shopify products and checked the public Klaviyo Reviews batch summary endpoint.

Goelia completed as a zero-row full catalog result. Public Klaviyo onsite widgets returned no active widgets. Public Klaviyo batch summary returned no products with public review_count. A direct per-product Klaviyo client_reviews endpoint spot check for the seed product also returned reviews: [] and review_count 0, despite the product page exposing Shopify review rating/count metafields.

Goelia output files were written under FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/goelia1995_com/. Final Goelia summary: products_scanned 1761, rows_written 0, full_catalog_scrape_complete true, warnings explain provider_public_review_feed_unavailable. The Goelia active claim was marked completed_no_public_review_feed and the aggregate non-Amazon summary was refreshed.
Said the chat thread is done and asked to update git, S3, and the transcripts table.
